prefer_paths: rg as last resort only if CodeGraph tools return nothing
payload.reason: Claude (Sonnet 4.6) fell back to grep on mcp_server.cpp to find tool names instead of using find_symbol → read_symbol. This wastes tokens even before the grep fires because the decision to grep is already made. PreToolUse hook added to block it, CLAUDE.md updated with explicit prohibition and ordered fallback ladder.
payload.title: Never use grep/cat/head/tail on source files — use CodeGraph MCP tools

payload.body:
All verified; 10/10 test suites pass (core/storage/scan/index/read/materialize/memory/graph/mcp/acceptance).

PART A — Coverage honesty (scanner.cpp). Every non-binary text file now gets a lightweight kind='file' node (8KB null-byte sniff skips binaries), not just C++ frontend files. No files-table row / line table / symbols for non-code — just a node so memories resolve to ANY path (.md, .yaml, configs). New prune_unseen_lightweight_file_nodes() drops stale lightweight nodes, first resetting their affects edges to (to_node=NULL, resolved=0) to mirror delete_source_file_projection and avoid dangling resolved edges. Result: record_decision affecting docs/NOTES.md returns empty unresolved_affects.

PART B1 — Source-text FTS for intent discovery. Added symbols.body column (storage.cpp); extended fts_symbols to 4 cols (name/qualified_name/signature/body) + updated symbols_ai/ad/au triggers; schema user_version 1->2 via migrate_v1_to_v2() (drops old fts+triggers, ALTER TABLE adds body, INSERT INTO fts_symbols('rebuild') for row-sync). indexer.cpp populates body via symbol_body_text(source, span). read_tools.cpp sanitize_fts_query switched from AND'd exact quoted tokens to OR + prefix (term*); search_symbols bm25 got a 4th body weight 0.5. VERIFIED: search_symbol('register mcp tool') returns tool_registry/handle_request/ToolDefinition (was []).

PART B2 — find_prior_incidents tool. read_tools.cpp find_memory_incidents() (FTS over existing fts_memories, joins memories->nodes, affected paths via affects edges with COALESCE(node.title, to_ref)); mcp_server.cpp find_prior_incidents_json + registration. Returns memory_type/created_at/title/body/affects.

PART C — Guards/tests. Doctor: new dup_file_nodes check + expected user_version 1->2 (also expect_schema_objects). test-mcp: added ids 13/14/15 (anon-namespace resolves as mcpstep::anon_helper; .md affects resolves; find_prior_incidents provenance), response count 12->15, separate codegraph_anon.cpp fixture so list_symbols id-11 count stays 2, plus docs/NOTES.md. test-index: FTS-after-delete assertion changed from MATCH 'gone' to row-parity ABS(symbols - fts_symbols)=0 because bodies now make fixture tokens collide with the test's own source. Made /proc/self/exe portable (fallback to build/codegraph) in command_test_mcp + acceptance_doctor_and_bench so suite runs on macOS.

SELF-HEALING INDEX FIX (indexer.cpp source_projection_is_current). Migration added body to already-indexed files, but the indexer skipped them as 'current' (content hash unchanged) so bodies stayed NULL — looked like it needed delete+rebootstrap. Fixed: is_current now also requires body IS NOT NULL, so a single ordinary `index` run backfills missing bodies (self-healing, generalizes to any future per-symbol column). Indexer always writes non-NULL body (empty string for zero-length span). Backfilled live DB: 412->422 symbols all with bodies via plain `index` (no delete). New/changed files were always incremental; only the one-time migration backfill was stuck.

OPEN / NEXT (v2 order): B is now done. Remaining: C-tier was folded in. Next per v2 plan: tiered reference/call graph (D). Also still open from earlier handoff: anon-namespace was already landed (verified). Consider: dogfooding review of the tool ergonomics is happening next — capture findings as decisions/corrections.

NOTE: nothing committed yet; working tree on main has the full diff (scanner/storage/storage.h/indexer/read_tools[.h]/mcp_server/main.cpp). Live MCP server restarted onto new binary (find_prior_incidents advertised).
payload.title: v2 A+B: coverage honesty + intent FTS + find_prior_incidents + self-healing index

payload.body:
Durable rule from dogfooding (2026-06-25). search_symbol must preserve "empty means absent, not missed" — it is the agent's FIRST call, so a junk hit is worse than no hit.

CONTEXT: B1 switched sanitize_fts_query from AND'd exact tokens to OR + prefix (term*) to enable intent queries. That fixed recall (search_symbol('register mcp tool') -> tool_registry) but regressed precision: a query touching one ubiquitous token returned near-zero-bm25 false positives. Evidence: 'codegraph guard hook permission grep' (about a Python hook, not a C++ symbol) returned bare namespace symbols at bm25 ~ -2e-06. Before OR it returned [].

RULES:
1. Exclude kind='namespace' from search_symbol unless the caller explicitly sets kind='namespace'. Namespace symbols span the whole file, so their FTS body matches almost any query — they are the dominant noise source and are never an intent-search target (find_symbol handles exact namespace lookup). Do NOT FTS-index whole-file/file-spanning bodies in general.
2. Apply a relevance floor: drop results whose bm25 score is within rounding distance of 0 (near-zero magnitude). These are single-common-token prefix artifacts, not matches. Real matches observed at |score| >= ~0.4; junk at ~1e-6. Threshold is heuristic and should be re-tuned once the benchmark harness exists.
3. Prefer a relative/coverage-based floor over a hard absolute one long-term (corpus-size independent). Absolute epsilon is the interim fix.

ALSO (related durable guidance, lower priority, captured for the next session via find_prior_incidents):
- find_prior_incidents should return a snippet + memory_id + provenance by default, not full bodies (a 4-match query returned ~4k tokens of full handoffs). Add full-body fetch on demand.
- get_memory_for_file must surface handoffs that affect the file, not only corrections/decisions (editing scanner.cpp + a handoff affecting it returned empty memory).
- Result shape token waste: drop duplicate file/path, round score, strip newlines in signatures.
- Build order: benchmark harness (tool-vs-rg tokens+success) BEFORE the reference/call graph; do not adopt embedding/semantic search until the benchmark proves synonym-gap is the dominant failure (today's failures were ranking+trust, fixable in FTS). Lightweight nodes for non-code were correct; do not add line tables/symbols for non-code without a concrete need. Searchable docs/config content (text-FTS over lightweight files) is a real gap: markdown is currently invisible to search.
payload.title: search_symbol relevance contract: trust over recall
